15.reason: The condition should be p >= 99 or p == 100 to check if the stack is full (since max capacity is 100, indices 0-99 are valid). Using p==99 only catches the exact moment when p is 99, but after pushing, p becomes 100, and the next push would overflow without detection. The correct check is if(p >= 99) or if(p == 100) before incrementing.

Using p==99 only catches the exact moment when p is 99, but after pushing, p becomes 100, and the next push would overflow without detection. The correct check is if(p >= 99) or if(p == 100) before incrementing.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition should be p >= 99 or p == 100 to check if the stack is full (since max capacity is 100, indices 0-99 are valid). Using p==99 only catches the exact full state but misses overflow when p is already 100 or more due to a previous error. Also, the reference program uses p<100 to allow push, so the error condition should be p >= 100.

8.reason: Missing condition to break on input -1, causing infinite loop and time limit exceeded.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing condition to break on input -1, causing infinite loop and time limit exceeded.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop never terminates because there is no condition to break when a == -1 (end-of-operation marker). This causes an infinite loop leading to Time Limit Exceeded.

52.reason: The loop lacks a break condition for input -1, causing an infinite loop (Time Limit Exceeded) when the end of operations marker is encountered.
